content.full_text:
June 11, 1976                                    A-96-503


Mr. Jess P. Telles, Jr.
Linneman, Burgess, Telles & Van Atta
Post Office Box 1364
Los Banos, California  93635

Dear Mr. Telles:

     This as in reply to your letter of May 25, 1976,
regarding the development of a Conflict of Interest Code
for the Laguna Water District. Pursuant to the Political
Reform Act of 1974, Government Code §87300 et seq., every
local government agency, without exception, is required to
adopt a Conflict of Interest Code. Thus, the Laguna Water
District will be required to develop such a Code.

     It was recently learned by this office, however, that
the jurisdiction of the Water District is solely within
Merced County. This means that the code reviewing body
for the District will be the County Board of Supervisors,
and not the Fair Political Practices Commission. Accord-
ingly, the March 22, 1976, notice regarding a June 10, 1976,
deadline for submission of the Laguna Water District Conflict
of Interest Code to the Commission is not applicable, and
the District will be notified by the Board of Supervisors
of its Code submission deadline.

     If you have further questions or require additional
information, please let us know.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr.
                                        Chief, Conflicts of Interest
                                        Division

/glb

OFFICES OF

LINNEMAN, BURGESS, TELLES & VAN ATTA

L.M. LINNEMAN
GEORGE W. BURGESS
JESS P. TELLES, JR.
C.E. VAN ATTA
JAMES E. LINNEMAN
EUGENE J. VIERRA
RALPH J. COOK

ATTORNEYS AT LAW

P.O BOX 156
1870 MARQUETTE STREET
DOS PALOS, CALIFORNIA 93620
(209) 392-2141

SAN DIEGO OFFICE
1310 SIXTH STREET
LOS BANOS, CALIFORNIA 93635
(209) 826-4911

312 WEST 180 STREET
MERCED, CALIFORNIA 95340
(209) 723-2137

PLEASE REPLY TO

Los Banos Office

May 25, 1976


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California 95814

RE:  Laguna Water District

Gentlemen:

The above captioned district has consulted our office
concerning the Conflict of Interest Code for the dis-
trict. In this connection, the Laguna Water District
is a landowner voting district; its boundaries include
only 428 acres; all of which is owned by the George
Antoine family; all the members of the board of direc-
tors of the district are also members of the Antoine
family. The only reason the district was formed was
to contract with the United States Department of the
Interior for a supplemental water supply.

In view of the foregoing facts, it would appear that
the adoption of a Conflict of Interest Code by the
district would be an added cost and expense which
would not be of a useful nature. Would you please
advise us, in view of the foregoing, if the district
can be exempt from the provisions requiring the adop-
tion of such a code by a public agency.

Thank you for an early reply.

Very truly yours,

LINNEMAN, BURGESS, TELLES & VAN ATTA

JESS P. TELLES, JR.

JPT:rj

BOX 1364 1316 SIXTH STREET LOS BANOS, CALIFORNIA 93635 (209) 826-4911 PLEASE REPLY TO Los Banos Office May 25, 1976 Fair Political Practices Commission 1100 "K" Street Sacramento, California 95814 RE: Laguna Water District Gentlemen: The above captioned district has consulted our office concerning the Conflict of Interest Code for the district. In this connection, the Laguna Water District is a landowner voting district; its boundaries include only 428 acres; all of which is owned by the George Antoine family; all the members of the board of directors of the district are also members of the Antoine family. The only reason the district was formed was to contract with the United States Department of the Interior for a supplemental water supply. Very truly yours, LINNEMAN, BURGESS, TELLES & VAN ATTA JESS P. TELLES, JR. JPT:rj
